sections.paragraph:
διαχωριζω 1 aor. διεχώρισα LXX; aor. pass. διεχωρίσθην LXX; pf. pass. ptc. διακεχωρισμένος LXX (s. χωρίζω; Aristoph. et al., X., Pla.; PTebt 802, 14; POxy 1673, 5; LXX; TestJob 38:3; Test12Patr; GrBar 16:13; Philo, Joseph.; Mel., P. 55, 407) to cause a distance to be put between objects or persons, separate τὶ ἀπό τινος someth. fr. someth. 1 Cl 33:3 (cp. Gen 1:4, 6f; Jos., Bell. 1, 535; TestJos 13:6). Mid. and pass. be separated, part, go away (mid. Diod S 4, 53, 4; pass. of a divorced woman Jos., Ant. 15, 259) ἀπό τινος (in a love charm PGM 12, 458f ποίησον τὸν δεῖνα διαχωρισθῆναι ἀπὸ τοῦ δεῖνος get so-and-so separated from so-and-so; Herm. Wr. 1, 11b; Gen 13:9, 11; Sus 13 Theod.) mid. Lk 9:33; pass. 1 Cl 10:4 (Gen 13:14).—DELG s.v. Χώρα. M-M.

aor. aor. = aorist

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

  1. pf. = perfect

ptc. ptc. = participle

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

Test12Patr Test12Patr = Testaments of the Twelve Patriarchs, with interpolations II b.c.–III a.d.—List 2

GrBar GrBar = Greek Baruch (Apocalypsis Baruchi Graece)—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

someth. someth. = something

  1. fr. = from

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

TestJos TestJos = Testament of Joseph, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

Theod. Theod. = Theodotion, revision of Greek OT, II a.d.—List 2, beg.

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 240
